macro_events.event_summary: A House Republican proposed a small-business tax cut to be paired with the GOP federal budget package.
primary_drivers: Lower effective tax rate for small-business-heavy customer bases increases discretionary spend and loan demand
Improved after-tax cash flow for pass-through entities boosts payroll/software/insurance spending
Legislative proposal only — not enacted — so impact is sentiment/positioning rather than earnings

beneficiaries.rationale: Paychex derives the majority of revenue from SMB payroll/HR services; lower small-business taxes free up cash for hiring and retention of payroll platforms, and historically Paychex trades as a proxy for SMB health.
Intuit's QuickBooks and TurboTax Small Business segments have direct SMB revenue exposure; tax cuts increase SMB formation and retention, expanding TAM for QuickBooks Online subscriptions.
Actually use LPRO — correcting: LendingTree/SMB lenders. Skipping this entry.
Open Lending and small-business-focused lenders see improved credit quality and higher originations when SMBs have more after-tax cash; however link is indirect.
Texas Capital and other SMB-heavy regional banks benefit from stronger small-business borrower balance sheets and potential loan demand pickup from reinvestment of tax savings.

headlines: House Republican proposes small-business tax cut to pair with GOP federal budget package

macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh, Trump's pick for Fed Chair, signals his approach to interest rates ahead of a potential leadership change at the Federal Reserve.
primary_drivers: Shift in monetary policy expectations under new Fed leadership
Perceived Fed independence / political alignment with Trump administration
Rate path repricing depending on whether Warsh signals hawkish or dovish tilt

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab's earnings are highly sensitive to the short-end rate path and deposit dynamics; a Warsh-led Fed perceived as willing to cut faster under Trump pressure reduces cash-sorting pressure and lifts client cash balances.

headlines: Trump’s Fed pick Kevin Warsh signals approach to rates — what a leadership change means for consumers
